claim: Title says bank-details-rejection-rate but body covers a broader withdrawal flow.
location: the title and the first paragraph of the Result section
finding: title scope and body scope diverge; reader will assume the body's numbers are the rejection-rate the title promised.
remediation: tighten title to match body scope, or expand body to match title.

claim: Methodology mixes fire-once and recurring events without naming the distinction.
finding: reader cannot tell which numerator is the per-user rate vs the per-event rate.
remediation: label each metric per-user or per-event.

claim: Denominator changes between table 1 and table 2 with no callout.
location: tables 1 and 2 in the Results section
finding: table 1 uses bank-detail-attempted as denominator; table 2 uses checkout-started — implied as the same population.
remediation: either harmonise or add a caveat row.

claim: Headline number has no comparison anchor.
finding: the 2.95% is reported without a control-period baseline; reader cannot judge significance.
remediation: add a baseline period or a control-arm comparison.

claim: Body hedges as 'comfortably above threshold' on a 0.3pp delta.
location: the Result section, around the comfortably claim
finding: hedge promotes a marginal delta to a confident framing.
remediation: replace 'comfortably' with the literal pp delta.

claim: TLDR claims a win; body documents a parity.
finding: the two surfaces disagree; reader's first impression diverges from the conclusion they'd reach reading the body.
remediation: rewrite TLDR to match the body's conclusion or vice versa.

claim: Result table is sorted by variant name instead of by effect size.
location: the Result table at the top of the Result section
finding: alphabetical ordering masks which arm carried the effect; reader scans top-down expecting strongest effect first.

claim: No mature-cohort scope stated alongside the headline rate.
finding: the rate is computed against an unspecified cohort window; reader cannot reproduce.
remediation: name the cohort scope and conversion window up front.

claim: Three consecutive blockquote callouts in the Caveat section dilute their signal.
finding: stacked callouts collapse into one wall the reader skims past.
remediation: merge into one callout or convert to bullets.

claim: Writeup has no explicit failure-mode discussion.
finding: without a pre-mortem block the reader cannot triangulate what would falsify the conclusion.
remediation: append a 'What would change this read' subsection.
